quote: Physicists rarely invent anything without first getting permission from a philosopher.
through_line: Rest and re-entry. The week's questions — about what systems owe to communities, what great writing does beneath the surface, how different minds perceive differently, and what happens when inquiry becomes a practice rather than a method — don't resolve on Sunday. They settle into the background, where they continue to work.

articles.title: The Jellies That Evolved a Different Way to Keep Time
articles.pitch: A March 2026 Quanta piece about a pea-sized jellyfish off the coast of Japan whose circadian clock runs on a 20-hour cycle — and which belongs to a class of animals that lost the genes that run circadian clocks in every other animal. Evolution found another path. A beautiful piece about the diversity of solutions to the same problem, which is also, quietly, a piece about neurodiversity.
